channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tech Companies' AI Push and Workforce Reductions: A Closer Look
item.description: Tech giants like Meta and Microsoft have recently announced significant layoffs alongside major investments in artificial intelligence (AI). This has raised questions about whether AI is driving these...

item.title: Joseph Tegbe Nominated as Nigeria’s Minister of Power: Background and Career Highlights
item.description: On Thursday, April 30th, President Bola Tinubu nominated Joseph Tegbe as Nigeria's new Minister of Power, a move set to strengthen ongoing reforms in the power sector. Tegbe's nomination is now with t...

item.title: Reform UK politicians highlight JCB pothole-fixing machines following £200,000 company donation
item.description: Several senior figures of Reform UK, including Nigel Farage, Lee Anderson, Robert Jenrick, Zia Yusuf, and Richard Tice, have publicly promoted the JCB PotHole Pro, a machine designed to repair pothole...

item.title: Police seize P12 million worth of undocumented cigarettes during inspection in Davao Oriental
item.description: Authorities confiscated smuggled cigarettes valued at around P12 million during a police operation conducted in Governor Generoso, Davao Oriental. The seizure happened on Friday after law enforcement ...

item.title: Durban City ends 17-year wait to lift Nedbank Cup with 2-1 win over TS Galaxy
item.description: Durban City have ended a 17-year Nedbank Cup drought after securing a 2-1 win over TS Galaxy at the Peter Mokaba Stadium in Polokwane on Saturday. The KZN side started the match strongly, dominating p...

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Reach Record Highs Amid Strong Corporate Earnings; Oil Prices Fall
item.description: The U.S. stock market opened May on a strong note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching all-time highs. The S&P 500 rose 0.29% to 7,230.12, while the Nasdaq climbed 0.89% to 25,114.44. Apple...

item.title: Bauchi Governor, Bala Mohammed Defects to Allied Peoples Movement Amid PDP Crisis
item.description: Governor Bala Mohammed of Bauchi State has officially defected from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) to the Allied Peoples Movement (APM). His decision comes after a series of internal disputes with...

item.title: Ahtisa Manalo Reflects on Her Journey and Inspires with Final MUPH Walk Message
item.description: Ahtisa Manalo, a prominent figure in the pageant world, took a moment during her final walk at Miss Universe Philippines 2026 to reflect on her growth and the lessons she learned throughout her journe...

item.title: 62-year-old man killed in motorcycle-ambulance collision in Bukidnon
item.description: A 62-year-old man tragically lost his life after his motorcycle collided with an ambulance in Manolo Fortich, Bukidnon on Thursday night. Authorities reported that the victim, whose identity has not y...

item.title: North West Debuts Edgy Look in Latest Music Video
item.description: North West, the 12-year-old daughter of Kim Kardashian and Kanye West, is once again making waves online with her latest music video. After entering the music scene in 2023 with a rap verse on her fat...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch apologises for inadvertent use of Bloody Sunday footage in social media post
item.description: Kemi Badenoch, the Conservative leader, has issued an apology following the use of footage from Bloody Sunday in social media posts that criticised a proposed bill on Northern Ireland's Troubles legac...

item.title: Impact of funding transitions on methadone access in Cape Town
item.description: People who use methadone to manage opioid dependency in Cape Town have reported inconsistent access to their medication, raising significant concerns about the stability of their recovery programmes. ...

item.title: Senior Police Officer Dismissed for Authorizing Cremation of Estranged Wife and Son Before Investigation Completion
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Senior Master Sergeant John Mollenido of the Philippine National Police has been dismissed from service after being found administratively liable for grave misconduct, neglect of...

item.title: Family Seeks Justice after 23-Year-Old Woman Dies Following Suspected Assault in Nairobi
item.description: A Nairobi family is grieving after 23-year-old Mercy Wanjiru Kimani tragically died following a suspected assault after attending a birthday party. Mercy, who had just returned from church, was invite...

item.title: Charles III's War Rhetoric and Britain's Long-Standing Hostility Toward Russia
item.description: The British political establishment has long held a hostile attitude toward Russia, dating back to the Crimean War of 1853. This obsession is evident in their constant efforts to provoke wars with Rus...

item.title: Mexican Officials Resign Following U.S. Drug Trafficking Indictments
item.description: The governor and a mayor in Mexico have stepped down after being indicted by U.S. authorities for alleged involvement in drug trafficking. This development follows a U.S. criminal investigation that l...

item.title: Anyango Jabalo Explains Her Relationship with Jaramogi Oginga and the Odinga Family
item.description: Tiktoker Anyango Jabalo, who has been publicly claiming to be one of Jaramogi Oginga's wives, has responded to Raila Odinga's sister, Pauline, after she refuted those claims. Pauline clarified that wh...

item.title: Grace Tame criticises NDIS funding cuts and approach to participant reduction
item.description: Grace Tame has spoken out on Crikey's Cut Through podcast about the Albanese government’s recent overhaul of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), which will see $15 billion cut over the ne...

item.title: Starmer suggests certain pro-Palestine demonstrations may be restricted
item.description: Labour leader Keir Starmer has indicated that, under specific circumstances, some pro-Palestine demonstrations in the UK might be prevented from taking place. While the details of which events or cond...

item.title: Three dead in Jammu bridge collapse, government suspends two engineers pending inquiry
item.description: In Jammu's Thuther area of Bantalab, a bridge under repair collapsed on Friday, resulting in three fatalities and multiple injuries. The bridge had developed structural cracks following the flash floo...
